rulings.Case Title: MALCOLM DRILLING COMPANY, INC. VS. SWINERTON WEBCOR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 3, 2024, line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT HAITONG INTERNATIONAL EQUITY INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT LIMITED'ss [FROM CONSOLIDATED CASE CGC-21-591319; CGC-21-594098 AND CGC-22-598031) MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Continued to December 13 2024 on the Court's own motion.= (501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SFHA HOUSING CORPORATION VS. JOHN DOE I ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order That The Truth Of Matters Be Deemed Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 3, 2024, line 5. DEFENDANT ANGELINA ARMOND's Motion For Order That The Truth Of Matters Be Deemed Admitted. Based on the opposition filed the by Plaintiff, and Plaintiff's insistence that this motion should have been filed on a 16-court day notice per CCP 1005, Plaintiff no longer wants to pursue a cause of action under CCP 1159 et seq , with discovery motion deadlines and notice requirements under CCP 1170.8, and wishes to proceed with this case as a general civil action. Therefore, this action is removed from the Summary unlawful detainer trial calendar in department 501 and shall be transferred to the Case Management and trial calendar in department 206. All further law and motion matters shall be noticed in department 501; all further discover matters to be noticed in department 302. The Court notes that this motion and the 5-day deadlines could have been avoided had Plaintiff timely and properly indicated its intent to no longer proceed with this action as a summary proceeding by filing an amended complaint or filing a motion to convert the UD action into a general civil case. Plaintiff's position appears to be a surprise to the Defendant, operating under UD laws and to the Court, having received the first indication of the Plaintiff's position in the motion to this motion. = (501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SONIA MALO VS. THEODORE PARODI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Order Setting Of Trial Date
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 3, 2024, line 6. PLAINTIFF SONIA MALO's Motion To Order Setting Of Trial Date. Plaintiff shall file an amended to the Second Amended Complaint compliant with CCP 1166(a)(3). Plaintiff may use UD-100 paragraph 3(a) for guidance as to the required information, i.e. street address, apt., no., city, zip code and county. Thereafter, Plaintiff may re-submit its request to set the case for trial filling paragraph 2 of form UD-150 using the same address that is listed in the Amendment to the Second Amended Complaint. = (501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 501 COLUMBUS, LLC VS. TAHREN LEIGH DENSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Of Defendant'S Motion To Vacate; Motion; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; Declaration Of Tahren Denson In Support; Declaration Of Jack Marschke In Support
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 3, 2024, line 9. DEFENDANT TAHREN DENSON's Motion For Reconsideration Of Defendant'S Motion To Vacate. DENIED. Moving party failed to carry their burden under CCP 1008. The Court notes that according to the Notice of Motion the Defendant purportedly seeks reconsideration of "Summary Judgment." rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION VS. KAHTOA YEUNG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motions, Second Motion To Quash, Points And Authortiy, And Motion For Continuance
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 3, 2024, line 8. DEFENDANT YEUNG TOA's Second Motion To Quash, And Motion For a Continuance. DENIED. The motion is not set for hearing in conformity with CCP 1167.4. Moving party failed to overcome the presumption under Evidence Code 647 afforded to registered process servers. There is no valid basis for continuance or appointment of a court appointed counsel. Defendant shall refer to the Notice to Defendant mailed to him by the Court on September 30, 2024 for legal resources available in UD actions. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
